plus_is_O.solution:
intros n m H. destruct n as [| n'].
  - destruct m as [| m'].
    + split. reflexivity. reflexivity.
    + discriminate H.
  - discriminate H.
plus_is_O.explanation: Destruct n; if n = S n', then n + m = 0 is impossible. If n = 0, destruct m similarly.

proj2.solution: intros P Q [HP HQ]. apply HQ.
proj2.explanation: Destruct the conjunction hypothesis to extract Q.

and_assoc.solution:
intros P Q R [HP [HQ HR]]. split.
  - split. apply HP. apply HQ.
  - apply HR.
and_assoc.explanation: Decompose P /\ (Q /\ R) and reassemble as (P /\ Q) /\ R.

mult_is_O.solution:
intros n m H. destruct n as [| n'].
  - left. reflexivity.
  - destruct m as [| m'].
    + right. reflexivity.
    + discriminate H.
mult_is_O.explanation: If n = 0, left disjunct holds. If n = S n', check m: if m = 0, right holds; otherwise n*m <> 0, contradiction.

or_commut.solution:
intros P Q [HP | HQ].
  - right. apply HP.
  - left. apply HQ.
or_commut.explanation: Destruct the disjunction and swap the sides.

not_implies_our_not.solution: intros P HNP Q HP. unfold not in HNP. apply HNP in HP. destruct HP.
not_implies_our_not.explanation: Apply ~P to the proof of P to get False, then destruct False to prove any Q.

double_neg_informal.solution: (* Informal proof: Assume P holds. We need to show ~~P, i.e., ~P -> False. So assume ~P (i.e., P -> False). But we have P, so applying ~P to P gives False. *)
double_neg_informal.explanation: This is an informal proof exercise; the comment explains the reasoning.

contrapositive.solution: intros P Q HPQ HNQ HP. apply HNQ. apply HPQ. apply HP.
contrapositive.explanation: Assume P -> Q, ~Q, and P. Apply P -> Q to P to get Q, then apply ~Q to Q for contradiction.

not_both_true_and_false.solution: intros P [HP HNP]. apply HNP. apply HP.
not_both_true_and_false.explanation: Destruct the conjunction to get P and ~P, then apply ~P to P to derive False.

not_PNP_informal.solution: (* Informal proof: Suppose P /\ ~P. Then we have both P and ~P. Since ~P means P -> False, applying it to P gives False. This is a contradiction, so ~(P /\ ~P). *)
not_PNP_informal.explanation: Informal proof exercise showing P /\ ~P leads to contradiction.

de_morgan_not_or.solution:
intros P Q H. split.
  - intro HP. apply H. left. apply HP.
  - intro HQ. apply H. right. apply HQ.
de_morgan_not_or.explanation: To show ~P, assume P and derive a contradiction via left disjunct. Similarly for ~Q via right disjunct.

not_S_pred_n.solution: intros H. assert (H0 : S (pred 0) = 0). { apply H. } simpl in H0. discriminate H0.
not_S_pred_n.explanation: Instantiate the universal with n=0. Then S (pred 0) = S 0 <> 0, contradiction.

nil_is_not_cons.solution:
intros X x xs H.
  assert (Hd : (fun l : list X => match l with [] => True | _ :: _ => False end) nil).
  { simpl. apply I. }
  rewrite H in Hd. simpl in Hd. apply Hd.
nil_is_not_cons.explanation: Use a discriminating function that maps nil to True and cons to False, then rewrite to derive False.

iff_refl.solution:
intros P. split.
  - intro HP. apply HP.
  - intro HP. apply HP.

iff_trans.solution:
intros P Q R [HPQ HQP] [HQR HRQ]. split.
  - intro HP. apply HQR. apply HPQ. apply HP.
  - intro HR. apply HQP. apply HRQ. apply HR.
iff_trans.explanation: Chain the implications in both directions.

or_distributes_over_and.solution:
intros P Q R. split.
  - intros [HP | [HQ HR]].
    + split. left. apply HP. left. apply HP.
    + split. right. apply HQ. right. apply HR.
  - intros [[HP | HQ] [HP' | HR]].
    + left. apply HP.
    + left. apply HP.
    + left. apply HP'.
    + right. split. apply HQ. apply HR.
or_distributes_over_and.explanation: Forward: P gives both disjuncts; Q/\R gives right side of each. Backward: case analysis on both conjuncts.

dist_not_exists.solution: intros X P H [x HNPx]. apply HNPx. apply H.
dist_not_exists.explanation: Assume exists x, ~P x. Destruct to get x and ~P x. But forall x, P x gives P x, contradiction.

dist_exists_or.solution:
intros X P Q. split.
  - intros [x [HP | HQ]].
    + left. exists x. apply HP.
    + right. exists x. apply HQ.
  - intros [[x HP] | [x HQ]].
    + exists x. left. apply HP.
    + exists x. right. apply HQ.
dist_exists_or.explanation: Split the existential with disjunction into disjunction of existentials and vice versa.

leb_plus_exists.solution:
intros n. induction n as [| n' IHn'].
  - intros m H. exists m. reflexivity.
  - intros m H. destruct m as [| m'].
    + discriminate H.
    + simpl in H. apply IHn' in H. destruct H as [x Hx]. exists x. simpl. rewrite Hx. reflexivity.
leb_plus_exists.explanation: Induction on n. Base: x = m. Step: destruct m; if 0, contradiction; otherwise use IH.

plus_exists_leb.solution:
intros n m [x Hx]. rewrite Hx. induction n as [| n' IHn'].
  - reflexivity.
  - simpl. apply IHn'.
plus_exists_leb.explanation: Rewrite m = n + x, then induct on n to show n <=? (n + x) = true.

In_map_iff.solution:
intros A B f l y. split.
  - induction l as [|x l' IHl'].
    + simpl. intros []. 
    + simpl. intros [H | H].
      * exists x. split. apply H. left. reflexivity.
      * destruct (IHl' H) as [x' [Hfx' Hin]]. exists x'. split. apply Hfx'. right. apply Hin.
  - intros [x [Hfx Hin]]. rewrite <- Hfx. apply In_map. apply Hin.
In_map_iff.explanation: Forward: induction on l, find the witness. Backward: use In_map theorem.

In_app_iff.solution:
intros A l. induction l as [|a' l' IH].
  - simpl. intros l' a. split.
    + intros H. right. apply H.
    + intros [[] | H]. apply H.
  - intros l'' a. simpl. rewrite IH. split.
    + intros [H | [H | H]].
      * left. left. apply H.
      * left. right. apply H.
      * right. apply H.
    + intros [[H | H] | H].
      * left. apply H.
      * right. left. apply H.
      * right. right. apply H.
In_app_iff.explanation: Induction on l, using IH to relate In in the tail to disjunctions.

All.solution:
Fixpoint All {T : Type} (P : T -> Prop) (l : list T) : Prop :=
  match l with
  | [] => True
  | x :: l' => P x /\ All P l'
  end.

Theorem All_In :
  forall T (P : T -> Prop) (l : list T),
    (forall x, In x l -> P x) <->
    All P l.
Proof.
  intros T P l. induction l as [| h t IH].
  - simpl. split.
    + intros _. apply I.
    + intros _ x [].
  - simpl. split.
    + intros H. split.
      * apply H. left. reflexivity.
      * apply IH. intros x Hin. apply H. right. apply Hin.
    + intros [HPh HAll] x [Heq | Hin].
      * rewrite <- Heq. apply HPh.
      * apply IH. apply HAll. apply Hin.
Qed.
All.explanation: All P l holds when P holds for every element. Defined as True for [] and P x /\ All P l' for x::l'.

combine_odd_even.solution:
Definition combine_odd_even (Podd Peven : nat -> Prop) : nat -> Prop :=
  fun n => if odd n then Podd n else Peven n.

Theorem combine_odd_even_intro :
  forall (Podd Peven : nat -> Prop) (n : nat),
    (odd n = true -> Podd n) ->
    (odd n = false -> Peven n) ->
    combine_odd_even Podd Peven n.
Proof.
  intros Podd Peven n Hodd Heven. unfold combine_odd_even.
  destruct (odd n) eqn:E.
  - apply Hodd. reflexivity.
  - apply Heven. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem combine_odd_even_elim_odd :
  forall (Podd Peven : nat -> Prop) (n : nat),
    combine_odd_even Podd Peven n ->
    odd n = true ->
    Podd n.
Proof.
  intros Podd Peven n Hcomb Hodd. unfold combine_odd_even in Hcomb.
  rewrite Hodd in Hcomb. apply Hcomb.
Qed.

Theorem combine_odd_even_elim_even :
  forall (Podd Peven : nat -> Prop) (n : nat),
    combine_odd_even Podd Peven n ->
    odd n = false ->
    Peven n.
Proof.
  intros Podd Peven n Hcomb Heven. unfold combine_odd_even in Hcomb.
  rewrite Heven in Hcomb. apply Hcomb.
Qed.
combine_odd_even.explanation: combine_odd_even checks odd n and dispatches to Podd or Peven accordingly.

logical_connectives.solution:
Theorem andb_true_iff : forall b1 b2:bool,
  b1 && b2 = true <-> b1 = true /\ b2 = true.
Proof.
  intros b1 b2. split.
  - intros H. destruct b1.
    + destruct b2.
      * split. reflexivity. reflexivity.
      * discriminate H.
    + discriminate H.
  - intros [H1 H2]. rewrite H1. rewrite H2. reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem orb_true_iff : forall b1 b2,
  b1 || b2 = true <-> b1 = true \/ b2 = true.
Proof.
  intros b1 b2. split.
  - intros H. destruct b1.
    + left. reflexivity.
    + simpl in H. right. apply H.
  - intros [H | H].
    + rewrite H. reflexivity.
    + rewrite H. destruct b1. reflexivity. reflexivity.
Qed.
logical_connectives.explanation: Boolean and/or reflect propositional and/or. Case analysis on the booleans.

eqb_neq.solution: intros x y. rewrite not_true_iff_false. rewrite eqb_eq. reflexivity.
eqb_neq.explanation: Chain not_true_iff_false and eqb_eq to relate =? false to <> .

eqb_list.solution:
Fixpoint eqb_list {A : Type} (eqb : A -> A -> bool)
                  (l1 l2 : list A) : bool :=
  match l1, l2 with
  | [], [] => true
  | [], _ :: _ => false
  | _ :: _, [] => false
  | x1 :: l1', x2 :: l2' => eqb x1 x2 && eqb_list eqb l1' l2'
  end.

Theorem eqb_list_true_iff :
  forall A (eqb : A -> A -> bool),
    (forall a1 a2, eqb a1 a2 = true <-> a1 = a2) ->
    forall l1 l2, eqb_list eqb l1 l2 = true <-> l1 = l2.
Proof.
  intros A eqb Heqb l1. induction l1 as [| x1 l1' IH].
  - intros l2. destruct l2 as [| x2 l2'].
    + simpl. split. intros _. reflexivity. intros _. reflexivity.
    + simpl. split. intros H. discriminate H. intros H. discriminate H.
  - intros l2. destruct l2 as [| x2 l2'].
    + simpl. split. intros H. discriminate H. intros H. discriminate H.
    + simpl. rewrite andb_true_iff. rewrite Heqb. rewrite IH. split.
      * intros [H1 H2]. rewrite H1. rewrite H2. reflexivity.
      * intros H. injection H as H1 H2. split. apply H1. apply H2.
Qed.
eqb_list.explanation: eqb_list compares element-wise using && . Proof by induction on l1 with case analysis on l2.

All_forallb.solution:
Fixpoint forallb {X : Type} (test : X -> bool) (l : list X) : bool :=
  match l with
  | [] => true
  | x :: l' => test x && forallb test l'
  end.

Theorem forallb_true_iff : forall X test (l : list X),
  forallb test l = true <-> All (fun x => test x = true) l.
Proof.
  intros X test l. induction l as [| x l' IH].
  - simpl. split. intros _. apply I. intros _. reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite andb_true_iff. rewrite IH. split.
    + intros [H1 H2]. split. apply H1. apply H2.
    + intros [H1 H2]. split. apply H1. apply H2.
Qed.
All_forallb.explanation: forallb tests all elements with &&. Equivalence to All follows by induction using andb_true_iff.

tr_rev_correct.solution:
Theorem tr_rev_correct : forall X, @tr_rev X = @rev X.
Proof.
  intros X. apply functional_extensionality. intros l.
  unfold tr_rev.
  assert (H : forall l1 l2 : list X, rev_append l1 l2 = rev l1 ++ l2).
  { intros l1. induction l1 as [| x l1' IH].
    - intros l2. reflexivity.
    - intros l2. simpl. rewrite IH. rewrite <- app_assoc. reflexivity. }
  rewrite H. rewrite app_nil_r. reflexivity.
Qed.
tr_rev_correct.explanation: Use functional extensionality and prove that rev_append l1 l2 = rev l1 ++ l2 by induction on l1.

excluded_middle_irrefutable.solution: intros P H. apply H. right. intro HP. apply H. left. apply HP.
excluded_middle_irrefutable.explanation: Assume ~(P \/ ~P). Then ~P holds (since assuming P gives P \/ ~P, contradicting H). But ~P gives P \/ ~P via right, also contradicting H.

not_exists_dist.solution:
intros EM X P H x. destruct (EM (P x)) as [HPx | HNPx].
  - apply HPx.
  - exfalso. apply H. exists x. apply HNPx.
not_exists_dist.explanation: Use excluded middle on P x. If P x, done. If ~P x, then exists x, ~P x contradicts H.

even_double_conv.solution:
intros n. induction n as [| n' IH].
  - exists 0. reflexivity.
  - destruct IH as [k Hk]. rewrite even_S.
    destruct (even n') eqn:E.
    + exists k. simpl. rewrite Hk. reflexivity.
    + exists (S k). simpl. rewrite Hk. reflexivity.
even_double_conv.explanation: Induction on n. Use even_S to flip the parity and adjust the witness k accordingly.

classical_axioms.solution:
(* This exercise asks to prove equivalence of five classical axioms plus excluded_middle.
   Here is a proof sketch of the circular chain:
   excluded_middle -> double_negation_elimination:
     Assume EM. Given ~~P, by EM either P or ~P. If P, done. If ~P, apply ~~P to ~P, contradiction.
   double_negation_elimination -> peirce:
     Assume DNE. Apply DNE to ~P. Assume ~P. Apply ((P->Q)->P) to (fun HP => ex_falso (apply ~P HP)).
   peirce -> consequentia_mirabilis:
     Apply Peirce with Q := False.
   consequentia_mirabilis -> implies_to_or:
     Assume CM. Given P->Q. Apply CM to show ~P \/ Q.
     Assume ~(~P \/ Q). Then P holds (by CM, since ~P gives left disjunct contradiction).
     So Q holds. Right. Contradiction.
   implies_to_or -> de_morgan_not_and_not:
     Given ~(~P /\ ~Q). From implies_to_or with (P->P), we get ~P \/ P. Case analysis.
   de_morgan_not_and_not -> excluded_middle:
     Apply de_morgan_not_and_not to ~(~P /\ ~~P). Since ~P /\ ~~P is always contradictory,
     we get P \/ ~P.
*)
classical_axioms.explanation: Optional 5-star exercise proving equivalence of classical axioms in a circular chain.
